channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Despite AI bubble fears, memory chip makers work to fill insatiable demand
item.description: Markets have been on edge about the AI investment boom, but earnings from the biggest U.S. memory chip maker, Micron, signal no end in sight to demand for the microchips at the heart of it all.
item.content:encoded: Markets have been on edge about the AI investment boom, but earnings from the biggest U.S. memory chip maker, Micron, signal no end in sight to demand for the microchips at the heart of it all.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: U.S. military works on building a better meal for the troops
item.description: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.content:encoded: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: Meta plans to release AI-powered prediction market app, documents show
item.description: The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."
item.content:encoded:
The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."

(Image credit: Kelly Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Star Fox Review: Can't quite teach an old Fox new tricks
item.description: The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.

(Image credit: James Mastromarino/Nintendo)
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
item.description: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."
item.content:encoded:
Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

(Image credit: Chris Hondros/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: AI and tech are trying to influence the midterm elections
item.description: The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.
item.content:encoded:
The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.

(Image credit: Natalie Behring)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Get with the times — here's what a 'Luddite' means today
item.description: It's often a derogatory term used to describe digital dinosaurs and technophobes. That wasn't always the case. NPR's Word of the Week looks back at the not so backwards-looking Luddites.
item.content:encoded:
It's often a derogatory term used to describe digital dinosaurs and technophobes. That wasn't always the case. NPR's Word of the Week looks back at the not so backwards-looking Luddites.

(Image credit: Manuel Orbegozo)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: Are Snap's $2,195 smart glasses the next big thing in tech?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Joanna Stern about the release of new augmented reality glasses from Snap and the future of wearable technology.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Joanna Stern about the release of new augmented reality glasses from Snap and the future of wearable technology.

(Image credit: Phillip Faraone)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Researchers find malware that may have aimed to slow down Iran's nuclear program
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers recently uncovered a cyber weapon that twisted math -- likely to slow down Iran's nuclear program.
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers recently uncovered a cyber weapon that twisted math -- likely to slow down Iran's nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Nick Fountain

item.title: Anthropic incident leaves confusion about Trump administration's AI regulation
item.description: There's been a lot of confusion and disruption about the legal authority the Trump administration has to suspend AI technology.
item.content:encoded: There's been a lot of confusion and disruption about the legal authority the Trump administration has to suspend AI technology.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram
